assertions.reason: Repo onboarding guidance documents install, safe env setup, dev startup, and canonical validation commands from the repository root.
Safe env onboarding is committed via .env.example while local env variants remain ignored in .gitignore.
Local artifact ignore rules cover env locals, Playwright outputs, and tsbuildinfo while not ignoring next-env.d.ts.
Review ownership is configured with a real maintainer handle and includes governance-critical paths.
Dependabot is configured in the GitHub-recognized path for weekly npm dependency updates at the repo root.
Bug and feature issue templates exist in GitHub-recognized paths and use repo-specific prompts.
The pull request template requires summary, validation checklist, related context, and risk notes tied to this repo workflow.
The validation workflow runs install, lint, typecheck, readiness scan, unit tests, browser tests, and build using real pnpm commands.
The Husky pre-commit hook calls the committed precommit:check script, which resolves to lint-staged plus typecheck in package.json.
README, package scripts, CI workflow, and Factory service manifest align on canonical lint, typecheck, test, readiness, and build command names.
Factory user-testing guidance defines the CLI surface as package scripts and repo-governance file checks without browser usage.
Factory operational artifacts remain repo-specific and aligned to the approved local smoke target on 127.0.0.1:3001.
Governance guidance and templates consistently reference reproducible repo-specific commands and validation expectations.
All required governance artifacts were present in expected GitHub or Factory paths during file existence checks.
Assigned governance assertions were validated read-only from the repository root using file inspection and lightweight CLI spot checks only.

frictions: Initial attempt to read global user-testing guidance at /Users/amankumarshrestha/.factory/library/user-testing.md failed because this repo stores the guidance under the repo-local .factory/library path.
A first services.yaml parsing spot-check using PyYAML failed because the yaml module is unavailable in the base Python 3.9 environment; a plain-text parser was used instead.
